Project 4

Visualising data about videos from the YouTube channels Vox and Vice

Akira Kalam

Introduction

I selected the YouTube channels Vox and Vice for comparison because both channels focus on educational content, including interviews and documentaries. I aim to explore any variations in viewer engagement between the two channels despite their similar content offerings.

Ideas before accessing the data

Before accessing the data for visualisation, I noticed that Vice’s popular videos tended to be more provocative and centred around crime, whereas Vox’s popular content was often the opposite. Additionally, I observed that recent videos from Vice generally had lower average views compared to Vox.

Process of Creating Data Visualisations

In creating my data visualisations, I began by utilising the YouTube Data API to create a description variable, as I believed understanding the content topics was crucial for comparison. I then used string functions to detect common topics between the two channels. Variables such as view count were chosen to measure viewer engagement, while the date_published variable allowed for analysis of trends over the years. Likes and video duration were also incorporated to examine the relationship between video length and viewer engagement.

For chart types, I selected geom_bar for the first visualisation to effectively distinguish between topics and align them with view counts. Geom_line was chosen for the second visualisation to illustrate trends in view counts over the years. Lastly, geom_col was utilised for the third visualisation to clearly display the average likes for videos of varying durations.

Challenges and Attempts

Throughout the process, I encountered challenges, particularly in creating the description variable using the YouTube Data API, which required numerous trial-and-error attempts. Additionally, I initially struggled with using the date_published variable to analyse trends due to its full date format. This presented difficulties in graphing specific trends over time.

Dynamic Data Story

I demonstrated creativity through the creation of my visualisations. I ensured consistency by adopting a purple theme for both the charts and titles, enhancing their visual appeal. This involved exploring the functions within the ‘theme()’ feature in R, which I discovered through resources like the R graph gallery. Additionally, incorporating images into my slides required me to learn how to use the ‘image_composite’ function, which wasn’t covered in class. Alongside incorporating the code taught in class, I had to seek out outside resources to achieve the desired results. Furthermore, I incorporated channel images into my slides, such as on the title slide, to enhance viewer recognition of the channels. In my third visualisation, I used ‘geom_text’ to elegantly display the channel names within the visual. Moreover, I had to learn how to prevent values from being displayed in scientific notation by utilising the ‘labels = scales::label_comma()’ function. Additionally, I concluded my presentation with a charming image, aligning it with the overall tone and content of the slides.

Learning Reflection

One important idea I gained from Module 4 is the significance of utilising string functions when mutating variables. These functions proved incredibly useful during my data exploration, allowing me to manipulate and extract valuable insights from text-based variables. By employing string functions, I enhanced the richness of my analysis and gained a deeper understanding of the dataset.

Regarding further exploration within data technologies, I am particularly curious about delving into the manipulation and exploration of date and time variables. This is an area that I initially lacked proficiency in at the beginning of my exploration. Learning how to work with date and time data effectively would enable me to uncover patterns, trends, and relationships within datasets, enhancing the depth and span of my analyses.

Appendix

library(tidyverse)
csv_file <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vQZfSbjU3ca71BVE1ezyifq-rNTv9tmsTkD2kULOIhpIShhPcxAagu4dovbpGWj-551exhDOS5v2L79/pub?output=csv"

## renaming the variables
youtube_data <- read_csv(csv_file) %>%
  rename(channel_name = 1,
         channel_id = 2,
         video_id = 3,
         thumbnail_url = 4,
         date_published = 5,
         view_count = 9,
         like_count = 10,
         comment_count = 11)

## mutating a variable based on the description variable that contains the following properties
mutated_youtube_data <- youtube_data %>%
  mutate(topic = case_when(
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")), 
               " politics | government | war | law | election ") ~ "politics",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " history | historian ") ~ "history",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " environment | ocean | sea | animal| climate | pollution | climate change | global warming | nature| earth| planet ") ~ "environment",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " crime | rape | drug | drugs | blood | murder | criminal | killer| scammer | scam | theft | terrorism | terrorist | hackers | harassment | sexual assault ") ~ "crime",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " health | disease | weight | sickness | illness | covid | pandemic | epidemic | cancer | doctors | medicine | medical ") ~ "health",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " documentary ") ~ "documentary",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " interview ") ~ "interview",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " faith | islam | muslim | christianity | christian | religion ") ~ "religion",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " art | artist | creative ") ~ "art",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " celebrity | celebrities | influencer | actor | actress | singer | sport | sports ") ~ "entertainment",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " film | movie | director | producer ") ~ "film",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " money | finance | economy | income | tax| taxes | cash | savings | investment ") ~ "finance",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " music | songs | singer | songwriter | producer ") ~ "music",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " AI | artificial intelligence | technology | computer | computers| mobile ") ~ "technology",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(paste0(" ", description, " ")),
               " business | companies | company | firms ") ~ "business",
    TRUE ~ "other"
  )) %>%
  ## mutating a variable based on the year_published variable that contains the following properties
  mutate(year_published = str_sub(date_published, 1,4) %>%
            parse_number()) %>%
  ## mutating a variable indicating if the video is from Vice or Vox
  mutate(channel_type = str_to_lower(channel_name) %>%
           str_remove_all("[[:punct:]]"))

## grouping youtube data by year and channel
grouped_data_one <- mutated_youtube_data %>%
  group_by(year_published, channel_name) %>%
  select(channel_name, view_count, year_published)

## grouping youtube data by channel type and calculating average likes and duration
grouped_data_two <- mutated_youtube_data %>%
  group_by(channel_type) %>%
  summarise(avg_likes = mean(like_count),
            avg_duration = mean(duration) / 60 %>% round())

## visualisation 1
mutated_youtube_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = view_count, y = topic)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill = channel_name),
           stat = "identity",
           position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(vars(channel_name),
             scales = "free_x") +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  labs(x = "Number of views from Youtube videos", y = "Topic of Youtube videos",
       title = "Vice vs Vox: Exploring YouTube Video Topic Popularity",
       subtitle = "Comparing the popularity of Youtube videos based on the topics of videos") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("@Vox" = "#A865B5", "@VICE" = "#743089")) + 
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(colour = "#85586F", face = 'bold'),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#E1AFD1"),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#E1AFD1"),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "#85586F", size = .2),
        panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "#85586F", size = .2),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "#85586F", face = 'bold', size = 10),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 6.5)) +
  ggsave("plot1.png", width = 6, height = 4, units =
           "in")


## visualisation 2
ggplot(grouped_data_one) +
  geom_line(aes(x = year_published, y = view_count, colour = channel_name)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(channel_name), 
             scales = "free") +
  labs(x = "Year Published", y = "Amount of views of Youtube videos",
       title = "YouTube Views Trend: Vox and Vice",
       subtitle = "Comparing the amount of views of Youtube videos by each year") +
  guides(colour = "none") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("@Vox" = "#A865B5", "@VICE" = "#743089")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(colour = "#85586F", face = 'bold'),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#E1AFD1"),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#E1AFD1"),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "#85586F", size = .2),
        panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "#85586F", size = .2),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "#85586F", face = 'bold', size = 10)) +
  ggsave("plot2.png", width = 6, height = 4, units =
           "in")


## visualisation 3
grouped_data_two %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = avg_duration, y = avg_likes)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = channel_type)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = channel_type),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), 
            colour = "white",
            size = 5,
            stat = "identity") + 
  guides(fill = "none") +
  labs(x = "Average Duration (minutes)", y = "Average Likes",
       title = "Comparing Video Duration to Average Likes for Vox and Vice", 
       subtitle = "Relationship of Video Duration and Average Likes") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("vox" = "#A865B5", "vice" = "#743089")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(color = "#85586F", face = 'bold'),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#E1AFD1"),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#E1AFD1"),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "#85586F", size = .2),
        panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "#85586F", size = .2),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "#85586F", face = 'bold', size = 10)) +
  ggsave("plot3.png", width = 6, height = 4, units =
           "in")
library(magick)

## colour theme/palette
my_colours <- c("#BD83CE", "#D895DA", "#EDB7ED", 
                "#E1AFD1","black", "white", "#85586F")


## reading the channel images
vox <- image_read("https://yt3.googleusercontent.com/ytc/AIdro_l--QV8BYUShB8KbNYts7M2fLYs45tCz9KA1AAKzKwyiO4=s900-c-k-c0x00ffffff-no-rj")
vice <- image_read("https://yt3.googleusercontent.com/Nv_KBl_Xisv3VhrhtxRU9X61FhknDm_76J5sO9TKT0z_60v61krrzcTNPl_WYe4_U4Cuw2KHeA=s900-c-k-c0x00ffffff-no-rj")

## appending the images together into one image with a border
channels <- image_append(c(vox, vice)) %>%
  image_scale(500)

## creating the title 
title <- image_blank(width = 1200,
                      height = 400,
                      color = my_colours[4]) %>%
  image_annotate(text = "Exploring YouTube Trends: \nA Comparative Analysis of Vox and Vice",
                 color = my_colours[5],
                 size = 30,
                 font = "trebuchet",
                 gravity = "north",
                 location = "+0+20")

## title slide
slide1 <- image_composite(image_scale(title), channels, offset = "+335+110")


## visualisation 1 plot
v1_plot <- image_read("plot1.png") %>%
  image_scale(600)

## text for visualisation 1
v1_text <- "Vox's health video got 60M views on YouTube, showing interest in health. Vice's crime video got 25M views, less than Vox's. This shows audience differences; engaging content matters." %>%
  str_wrap(50)

## creating the slide for visualisation 1
v1 <- image_blank(width = 600,
                  height = 400,
                  color = my_colours[4]) %>%
  image_annotate(text = v1_text,
                 color = my_colours[5],
                 size = 20,
                 font = "trebuchet",
                 gravity = "west",
                 location = "+50+10")

## slide 2
slide2 <- c(v1, v1_plot) %>%
  image_append(stack = FALSE)


## visualisation 2 plot
v2_plot <- image_read("plot2.png") %>%
  image_scale(600)

## text for visualisation 2
v2_text <- "Vox peaked in 2014, averaging 5-10M views since 2017. Vice's peak was in 2013, with 5M views from 2015-2020, but spiked to almost 20M in 2022." %>%
  str_wrap(50)

## creating the slide for visualisation 2
v2 <- image_blank(width = 600,
                  height = 400,
                  color = my_colours[4]) %>%
  image_annotate(text = v2_text,
                 color = my_colours[5],
                 size = 20,
                 font = "trebuchet",
                 gravity = "west",
                 location = "+50+10")

# slide 3
slide3 <- c(v2, v2_plot) %>%
  image_append(stack = FALSE)


## visualisation 3 plot
v3_plot <- image_read("plot3.png") %>%
  image_scale(600)

## text for visualisation 3
v3_text <- "Vox typically receives over 75k likes for videos ranging between 1 to 6 minutes, whereas Vice receives more than 25k likes for videos ranging from 6.5 to 8 minutes." %>%
  str_wrap(50)

## creating the slide for visualisation 3
v3 <- image_blank(width = 600,
                  height = 400,
                  color = my_colours[4]) %>%
  image_annotate(text = v3_text,
                 color = my_colours[5],
                 size = 20,
                 font = "trebuchet",
                 gravity = "west",
                 location = "+50+10")

# slide 4
slide4 <- c(v3, v3_plot) %>%
  image_append(stack = FALSE)


## text for final slide
final_text <- "In summary, comparing viewer habits of Vox and Vice shows their strategies. Vox's consistent high viewership, especially for shorter videos, suggests strong audience interest. Also, Vox's health video with 60 million views indicates interest in health topics. On the other hand, Vice's viewership peaks in 2013 and 2022 suggest changing preferences. Adapting content to these trends is crucial for keeping audiences engaged." %>%
  str_wrap(50)

## creating the final slide
final <- image_blank(width = 1200,
                     height = 400,
                     color = my_colours[4]) %>%
  image_annotate(text = final_text,
                 color = my_colours[5],
                 size = 22,
                 font = "trebuchet",
                 gravity = "west",
                 location = "+50+10")

## cute image for the final slide
hello_kitty <- image_read("hello_kitty_fs.png") %>%
  image_scale(400)

## slide 5
slide5 <- image_composite(image_scale(final), hello_kitty, offset = "+600+5")


## slides put together
slides <- c(rep(slide1, 5), rep(slide2, 5), rep(slide3, 5), rep(slide4, 5), rep(slide5, 5))

## final animation
animated_slides <- image_animate(slides, fps = 1)

## save animation
image_write(animated_slides, "data_story.gif")
